---
title: "AgentCOP — Background Documentation"
subtitle: "The data-protection pipeline: flow, components, and contracts"
date: 2026-06-06
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Why this exists

Frontier cloud models give the best answers, but much of the data an enterprise would
send them is **legally or commercially un-sendable**: regulated PHI/PII (HIPAA), operational-
technology secrets (controller network config, credentials, robot programs), trade secrets,
and export-controlled technical data.

AgentCOP's bet: **answer locally when you can, and only escalate to the cloud once a gate has
confirmed the data is safe — or made it safe.** The user gets frontier-grade answers; the
sensitive bits never leave.

> Note: in this hackathon build, the "local" small models are simulated via cloud APIs
> (OpenRouter / Gemini). The design is model-agnostic, so a true on-prem model drops in later.

## The end-to-end flow

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[User prompt + documents from Box via MCP] --> B[Small 'local' LLM drafts an answer]
    B --> C{Hallucination check}
    C -- trustworthy --> D[Return local answer · STOP]
    C -- not trustworthy --> E[[POLICY AGENT · local]]
    E --> F{Contains proprietary data?}
    F -- no --> G[Send prompt + data to frontier cloud model · STOP]
    F -- uncertain / unreadable --> H[Human review · STOP]
    F -- yes --> I[Data massager / redactor]
    I --> E
    style E fill:#1b5e20,stroke:#4caf50,color:#fff
    style I fill:#4a148c,stroke:#ba68c8,color:#fff
```

The whole chain is wired in repo-root **`orchestrator.py`**. The Box retrieval and
hallucination stages need live credentials, so the orchestrator can also start at the
policy gate from a local document — which is the part that runs fully offline-capable.

## The components

### Box retrieval + local LLM — `box_openrouter_pipeline.py` *(Zack)*
Lists files in a Box folder, lets a planner model pick the relevant ones, downloads and
extracts their text (PDF/DOCX/CSV/XLSX/…), and asks a small "local" model to answer using
that content. Output: a draft `answer`.

### Hallucination check — `hallucination_detector.py` *(Abhiyan & Nav)*
Decides whether the local answer can be trusted. Domain-aware:

- **Robotics:** any cited error code must exist in the manual; an invented code = hallucination.
- **Healthcare follow-up:** a follow-up date must be present, valid, and in the future.

If the answer is trustworthy → **stop and return it** (no cloud, no risk). If not → **escalate**
to the policy agent. *This escalation is the entry point of our scope.*

### Policy Agent — `policy-agent/` (the gate) *(Ammar)*
Runs locally and decides whether prompt + data may be sent to the cloud. Two stages:

1. **Classify** (`agent/classifier_prompt.md`) — route the prompt+data to a use-case using
   signals in `policies/registry.yaml`. Low confidence / unknown / mixed-domain → `ESCALATE`.
2. **Evaluate** (`agent/evaluator_prompt.md`) — apply that case's `policies/<case>/policy.md`
   and emit a structured verdict.

**Intake** (`src/intake.py`) is format-flexible: PDFs (text extraction), controller-backup
*folders* (walks the tree, extracts text from XML/cfg/txt, flags binaries as opaque), raw text,
or metadata-only. Anything it can't read is recorded so the agent can escalate rather than
assume safety.

A model-independent guard deterministically **ESCALATEs** any attachment whose contents can't
be extracted — the unreadable case never depends on model strength.

### Data massager / redactor — `redact-healthcare.py` *(Emma)*
Owns *what* counts as PHI and *how* it's removed (rules in `rules/healthcare.yaml`, HIPAA Safe
Harbor). It performs **true redaction** (removes the underlying glyphs, not a black box over
them), writes placeholder tokens, then **re-scans the output and fails closed** — it will not
emit a document that still contains detectable PHI. Clinical content (diagnoses, meds, ages < 90)
is deliberately preserved so the cloud model can still do useful work.

## The verdict contract

`agent/schema/verdict.schema.json` — a three-state decision plus the findings that justify it:

| Verdict | Meaning | Next |
|--------|---------|------|
| `ALLOW` | no proprietary data | forward to frontier cloud model |
| `BLOCK` | proprietary data present | hand `findings[]` to the redactor |
| `ESCALATE` | can't confirm safety | human review |

Each finding carries `category`, `location` (field/file/snippet), `severity`, `rationale`, and a
`redaction_hint` (`mask` / `drop` / `generalize`) — enough for the redactor to act without
re-discovering everything.

## The integration insight: contracts between components

Two agreements make the **BLOCK → redact → re-check** loop actually terminate:

1. **Redaction-token awareness.** The redactor replaces PHI with placeholders
   (`[PATIENT]`, `[MRN]`, `[DOB]`, `[PROVIDER]`, `[FACILITY]`, year-only dates). Initially the
   policy agent saw `[MRN]` and reported "MRN detected" — re-blocking the redactor's own clean
   output indefinitely. The fix (in `evaluator_prompt.md` and healthcare `policy.md` §5b): treat
   those tokens as *already neutralized*, not findings. With that shared vocabulary, a redacted
   document re-checks to `ALLOW` and the loop converges.

2. **Massager coverage is explicit.** A redactor exists only for `healthcare` PDFs today, so an
   `industrial_ot` BLOCK has nothing to clean it — the orchestrator routes that to **human review**
   rather than failing silently.

## Running it

```bash
# one-time
pip install -r policy-agent/requirements.txt    # + PyMuPDF for the redactor
cp policy-agent/.env.example policy-agent/.env   # add OPENROUTER_API_KEY

# policy agent on its own (synthetic fixtures, offline)
python policy-agent/harness/run_validation.py --provider mock

# full pipeline, end to end (capable evaluator for the re-check)
python orchestrator.py --provider openrouter --model google/gemini-2.5-flash \
  --prompt "Attached is a patient discharge summary, find any follow-ups." \
  --doc data/healthcare/01_discharge_heart_failure_Okafor.pdf
```

Expected: `BLOCK` (PHI) → redactor `CLEAR` → `ALLOW` → `SEND_TO_CLOUD` (the **redacted** doc).

## Worked examples (real runs)

These are actual outputs, referenced so the deck doesn't need a live demo. (Patient data is synthetic.)

**Healthcare — `data/healthcare/01_discharge_heart_failure_Okafor.pdf`**, prompt *"find any follow-ups"*:

```
loop 1: BLOCK  (14 PHI findings)  → redactor: 50 redactions, CLEAR
loop 2: ALLOW                      → SEND_TO_CLOUD (redacted doc)
```

Sample findings: HC-1 name `Okafor, Emmanuel C.`, HC-2 MRN `NL-448821`, HC-3 DOB `09/14/1951`,
HC-7 attending `Dr. Priya Mehta`. Redaction audit at `output/01_discharge_heart_failure_Okafor.audit.json`.

**Industrial/OT — `…/6640_2-14-22_6.05restore/`**, prompt *"check the errors on the log…"*:

```
verdict: BLOCK  (industrial_ot, 49 findings)  →  no OT massager yet  →  HUMAN_REVIEW
```

Sample findings: OT-1 hostname `DESKTOP-9T2SPJS` (`rsmeta.xml`), OT-1 PROFINET addresses
(`HOME/ippnio.xml`), OT-3 serial `6640-501456` (`system.xml`), OT-2/3 UAS grants + key
(`uas_application_grants.xml`, `key.id`) — none of which were hard-coded in the policy.

Committed sample verdicts: `policy-agent/examples/verdicts/*.verdict.json`.

## Validation & limitations

- **9/9** validation (synthetic + real local data); every real backup/PDF correctly blocked, the
  ambiguous/opaque case escalates, de-identified content is allowed.
- **Model strength:** the small local-class default (`llama-3.1-8b`) is reliable for the initial
  BLOCK but over-flags already-redacted docs; the loop converges with a stronger evaluator
  (e.g. `gemini-2.5-flash`). A two-tier "small gate, capable verifier" split is the natural answer.
- **Coverage:** healthcare redaction only; `industrial_ot` redactor is the next piece.
- **Fail-safe posture:** unknown case, unreadable data, or low confidence all route to a human —
  the system never guesses "safe."

## Next steps

- An `industrial_ot` massager (mask network/credentials, generalize trajectories).
- A shared config (one `.env`, one confidence threshold) across all four components.
- Aligning the hallucination detector's domain labels with the policy agent's case ids.
